user_query:
I'm assembling an op-ed style report that analyzes recurring themes of censorship and propaganda across my recent film reviews. Please work entirely within this workspace and produce two outputs based on the review files under input/reviews and the configuration at config/report.yaml.

Requirements:
1) Inspect the input/reviews directory to discover all Markdown review files. Each file has YAML front matter with title, year, rating (1–5), and tags (list). Use all and only the .md files in that directory as inputs for the analysis and for the "Data sources" section of the report.

2) Modify the configuration file at config/report.yaml as follows, keeping other keys unchanged:
   - Set track_tags to exactly ["censorship", "state_media", "propaganda"].
   - Set output_dir to "output".
   The report and stats must reflect these updated values. Commit the edit to the file as part of the deliverables.

3) Compute the following aggregates from the discovered reviews:
   - total_reviews: count of review files used.
   - ratings_count: counts of each rating value observed.
   - average_rating: average of all ratings. Include sum and count so it can be recomputed.
   - tag_frequency: frequency of every tag across all reviews.
   - sensitive_tag_counts: frequency for each tag listed in track_tags (from the updated config).

4) Create output/stats/summary.json with this structure:
   {
     "config_used": {"report_title": <from config>, "author": <from config>, "track_tags": [..]},
     "total_reviews": <int>,
     "ratings_count": {"1": <int>, "2": <int>, "3": <int>, "4": <int>, "5": <int>},
     "average_rating": {"value": <float rounded to 2 decimals>, "sum_ratings": <int>, "count": <int>},
     "tag_frequency": {"<tag>": <int>, ...},
     "sensitive_tag_counts": {"censorship": <int>, "state_media": <int>, "propaganda": <int>}
   }

5) Write an editorial report to output/report/op_ed.md that:
   - Begins with a YAML front matter block including keys: title (use report_title from config), author (from config), track_tags (from the updated config), total_reviews (match summary.json), and average_rating (the same rounded value you put in summary.json).
   - Includes sections titled Overview, Sensitive Themes, Film list by sensitive tag, and Data sources.
     • Overview: a short paragraph reflecting the numbers (e.g., overall counts and averages) without referencing any external sources.
     • Sensitive Themes: list each track tag with its count as computed (must match summary.json).
     • Film list by sensitive tag: for each tag in track_tags, list the films (Title (Year) — rating X) that contain that tag.
     • Data sources: bullet list of the basenames of all input review files used (exact filenames under input/reviews).

Consistency constraints:
- The counts and averages in op_ed.md must match the values in output/stats/summary.json.
- Only reviews found under input/reviews should be used. Do not invent or omit files.
- Place outputs precisely at output/stats/summary.json and output/report/op_ed.md, using the updated output_dir in config/report.yaml.

Deliverables:
- Modified config/report.yaml reflecting the new track_tags and output_dir.
- output/stats/summary.json with the specified structure.
- output/report/op_ed.md with the specified sections and YAML front matter. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
